reasoning: The prompt asks if the 1-year real interest rate estimate has increased compared to its value on 2025-10-26 by two resolution dates: 2025-11-25 and 2026-01-24. According to our fetch_ts_fred data, the value for October 2025 is currently approximately 0.97. A web search confirmed there was a publication by the Cleveland Fed on October 24, 2025, meaning that on October 26, the base value was approximately 0.97, overriding the slightly older quoted value of 1.193. By November 25, the November estimate (currently at 1.00) would have been the latest available. Because 1.00 > 0.97, the first resolution date is very likely to resolve as YES. By January 24, 2026, the latest available estimate would be either January (0.86) or December (-0.14). Both of these values are well below 0.97, making the second resolution date very likely a NO. To ensure compliance with the instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, the second probability is placed slightly closer to 0.5 than the first probability (|0.20 - 0.5| = 0.30 < |0.82 - 0.5| = 0.32).
